items.headline: City Council addresses affordable housing protections when subsidies end
items.summary: The council reviewed new policies to maintain affordability restrictions on housing units if project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This affects residents in subsidized affordable units and determines whether those homes remain affordable long-term.

items.headline: Airport baggage system gets major upgrade agreement
items.summary: The council approved a fifth amendment to the baggage handling conveyor system contract with Vanderlande Industries at San José Airport. This signals ongoing modernization of airport infrastructure that affects travelers and airport operations.

items.headline: City approves storm sewer master planning expansion
items.summary: The council amended its agreement with Wood Rodgers for a city-wide master plan of the storm sewer system. This planning effort could influence future flood prevention, drainage improvements, and infrastructure priorities across San José.

items.headline: Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development adds 4 new homes to Altino Boulevard
items.summary: Four single-family homes permitted in the Comm Hill Phase 3.1 development along Altino Boulevard, ranging from $487K-$573K. This represents continued residential infill in an established neighborhood development project.

items.headline: Sherly Place development brings 3 new homes to the area
items.summary: Three single-family homes permitted at Sherly Place (lots 2-4), priced $496K-$570K. Part of a small-scale residential infill project adding modest housing stock.

items.headline: City Council reviews FY 2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a study session to examine planned capital improvements for the fiscal year ahead, which typically includes major infrastructure, facility, and development projects. This preview session allows council to discuss priorities before formal approval.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced changes to Chapter 19.71 of the municipal code governing residential tenant protections. The specific amendments were not detailed in the agenda, but this ordinance affects housing rights and protections for renters in Sunnyvale.
